ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Nun. By the pen and what they inscribe,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Nũn. By the pen and what everyone writes!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: You are not, [O Muhammad], by the favor of your Lord, a madman.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: By the grace of your Lord, you ˹O Prophet˺ are not insane.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, for you is a reward uninterrupted.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: You will certainly have a never-ending reward.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, you are of a great moral character.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And you are truly ˹a man˺ of outstanding character.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So you will see and they will see
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Soon you and the pagans will see,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Which of you is the afflicted [by a devil].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: which of you is mad.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعۡلَمُ بِمَن ضَلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ وَهُوَ أَعۡلَمُ بِٱلۡمُهۡتَدِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna rabbaka huwa aAAlamu biman dalla AAan sabeelihi wahuwa aAAlamu bialmuhtadeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who has gone astray from His way, and He is most knowing of the [rightly] guided.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely your Lord ˹alone˺ knows best who has strayed from His Way and who is ˹rightly˺ guided.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then do not obey the deniers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So do not give in to the deniers.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They wish that you would soften [in your position], so they would soften [toward you].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They wish you would compromise so they would yield ˹to you˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And do not obey every worthless habitual swearer
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And do not obey the despicable, vain oath-taker,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [And] scorner, going about with malicious gossip -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: slanderer, gossip-monger,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: A preventer of good, transgressing and sinful,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: withholder of good, transgressor, evildoer,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Cruel, moreover, and an illegitimate pretender.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: brute, and—on top of all that—an illegitimate child.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Because he is a possessor of wealth and children,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Now, ˹simply˺ because he has been blessed with ˹abundant˺ wealth and children,

ayahs.textArabic: إِذَا تُتۡلَىٰ عَلَيۡهِ ءَايَٰتُنَا قَالَ أَسَٰطِيرُ ٱلۡأَوَّلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Itha tutla AAalayhi ayatuna qala asateeru alawwaleena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, "Legends of the former peoples."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: whenever Our revelations are recited to him, he says, “Ancient fables!”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: We will brand him upon the snout.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We will soon mark his snout.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّا بَلَوۡنَٰهُمۡ كَمَا بَلَوۡنَآ أَصۡحَٰبَ ٱلۡجَنَّةِ إِذۡ أَقۡسَمُواْ لَيَصۡرِمُنَّهَا مُصۡبِحِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna balawnahum kama balawna ashaba aljannati ith aqsamoo layasrimunnaha musbiheena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, We have tried them as We tried the companions of the garden, when they swore to cut its fruit in the [early] morning
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, We have tested those ˹Meccans˺ as We tested the owners of the garden—when they swore they would surely harvest ˹all˺ its fruit in the early morning,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Without making exception.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: leaving no thought for Allah’s Will.

ayahs.textArabic: فَطَافَ عَلَيۡهَا طَآئِفٞ مِّن رَّبِّكَ وَهُمۡ نَآئِمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fatafa AAalayha taifun min rabbika wahum naimoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So there came upon the garden an affliction from your Lord while they were asleep.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then it was struck by a torment from your Lord while they slept,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And it became as though reaped.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: so it was reduced to ashes.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they called one another at morning,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then by daybreak they called out to each other,

ayahs.textArabic: أَنِ ٱغۡدُواْ عَلَىٰ حَرۡثِكُمۡ إِن كُنتُمۡ صَٰرِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ani ighdoo AAala harthikum in kuntum sarimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Saying], "Go early to your crop if you would cut the fruit."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹saying,˺ “Go early to your harvest, if you want to pick ˹all˺ the fruit.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So they set out, while lowering their voices,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So they went off, whispering to one another,

ayahs.textArabic: أَن لَّا يَدۡخُلَنَّهَا ٱلۡيَوۡمَ عَلَيۡكُم مِّسۡكِينٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: An la yadkhulannaha alyawma AAalaykum miskeenun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Saying], "There will surely not enter it today upon you [any] poor person."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: “Do not let any poor person enter your garden today.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they went early in determination, [assuming themselves] able.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And they proceeded early, totally fixated on their purpose.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But when they saw it, they said, "Indeed, we are lost;
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But when they saw it ˹devastated˺, they cried, “We must have lost ˹our˺ way!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Rather, we have been deprived."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In fact, we have been deprived ˹of our livelihood˺.”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ أَوۡسَطُهُمۡ أَلَمۡ أَقُل لَّكُمۡ لَوۡلَا تُسَبِّحُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala awsatuhum alam aqul lakum lawla tusabbihoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The most moderate of them said, "Did I not say to you, 'Why do you not exalt [Allah]?' "
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The most sensible of them said, “Did I not urge you to say, ‘Allah willing.’?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ سُبۡحَٰنَ رَبِّنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا ظَٰلِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo subhana rabbina inna kunna thalimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "Exalted is our Lord! Indeed, we were wrongdoers."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They replied, “Glory be to our Lord! We have truly been wrongdoers.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then they approached one another, blaming each other.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then they turned on each other, throwing blame.

ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo ya waylana inna kunna tagheena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "O woe to us; indeed we were transgressors.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They said, “Woe to us! We have certainly been transgressors.

ayahs.textArabic: عَسَىٰ رَبُّنَآ أَن يُبۡدِلَنَا خَيۡرٗا مِّنۡهَآ إِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا رَٰغِبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: AAasa rabbuna an yubdilana khayran minha inna ila rabbina raghiboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Perhaps our Lord will substitute for us [one] better than it. Indeed, we are toward our Lord desirous."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We trust our Lord will give us a better garden than this, ˹for˺ we are indeed turning to our Lord with hope.”

ayahs.textArabic: كَذَٰلِكَ ٱلۡعَذَابُۖ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلۡأٓخِرَةِ أَكۡبَرُۚ لَوۡ كَانُواْ يَعۡلَمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kathalika alAAathabu walaAAathabu alakhirati akbaru law kanoo yaAAlamoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Such is the punishment [of this world]. And the punishment of the Hereafter is greater, if they only knew.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: That is the ˹way of Our˺ punishment ˹in this world˺. But the punishment of the Hereafter is certainly far worse, if only they knew.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ لِلۡمُتَّقِينَ عِندَ رَبِّهِمۡ جَنَّٰتِ ٱلنَّعِيمِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna lilmuttaqeena AAinda rabbihim jannati alnnaAAeemi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, for the righteous with their Lord are the Gardens of Pleasure.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, the righteous will have the Gardens of Bliss with their Lord.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then will We treat the Muslims like the criminals?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Should We then treat those who have submitted like the wicked?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: What is [the matter] with you? How do you judge?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: What is the matter with you? How do you judge?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or do you have a scripture in which you learn
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or do you have a scripture, in which you read

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That indeed for you is whatever you choose?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: that you will have whatever you choose?

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ لَكُمۡ أَيۡمَٰنٌ عَلَيۡنَا بَٰلِغَةٌ إِلَىٰ يَوۡمِ ٱلۡقِيَٰمَةِ إِنَّ لَكُمۡ لَمَا تَحۡكُمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am lakum aymanun AAalayna balighatun ila yawmi alqiyamati inna lakum lama tahkumoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or do you have oaths [binding] upon Us, extending until the Day of Resurrection, that indeed for you is whatever you judge?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or do you have oaths binding on Us until the Day of Judgment that you will have whatever you decide?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Ask them which of them, for that [claim], is responsible.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Ask them ˹O Prophet˺ which of them can guarantee all that.

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ لَهُمۡ شُرَكَآءُ فَلۡيَأۡتُواْ بِشُرَكَآئِهِمۡ إِن كَانُواْ صَٰدِقِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am lahum shurakao falyatoo bishurakaihim in kanoo sadiqeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or do they have partners? Then let them bring their partners, if they should be truthful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or do they have associate-gods ˹supporting this claim˺? Then let them bring forth their associate-gods, if what they say is true.

ayahs.textArabic: يَوۡمَ يُكۡشَفُ عَن سَاقٖ وَيُدۡعَوۡنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ فَلَا يَسۡتَطِيعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yawma yukshafu AAan saqin wayudAAawna ila alssujoodi fala yastateeAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The Day the shin will be uncovered and they are invited to prostration but the disbelievers will not be able,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Beware of˺ the Day the Shin ˹of Allah˺ will be bared, and the wicked will be asked to prostrate, but they will not be able to do so,

ayahs.textArabic: خَٰشِعَةً أَبۡصَٰرُهُمۡ تَرۡهَقُهُمۡ ذِلَّةٞۖ وَقَدۡ كَانُواْ يُدۡعَوۡنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ وَهُمۡ سَٰلِمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: KhashiAAatan absaruhum tarhaquhum thillatun waqad kanoo yudAAawna ila alssujoodi wahum salimoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. And they used to be invited to prostration while they were sound.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: with eyes downcast, totally covered with disgrace. For they were ˹always˺ called to prostrate ˹in the world˺ when they were fully capable ˹but they chose not to˺.

ayahs.textArabic: فَذَرۡنِي وَمَن يُكَذِّبُ بِهَٰذَا ٱلۡحَدِيثِۖ سَنَسۡتَدۡرِجُهُم مِّنۡ حَيۡثُ لَا يَعۡلَمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fatharnee waman yukaththibu bihatha alhadeethi sanastadrijuhum min haythu la yaAAlamoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So leave Me, [O Muhammad], with [the matter of] whoever denies the Qur'an. We will progressively lead them [to punishment] from where they do not know.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So leave to Me ˹O Prophet˺ those who reject this message. We will gradually draw them to destruction in ways they cannot comprehend.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And I will give them time. Indeed, My plan is firm.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I ˹only˺ delay their end for a while, but My planning is flawless.

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ تَسۡـَٔلُهُمۡ أَجۡرٗا فَهُم مِّن مَّغۡرَمٖ مُّثۡقَلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am tasaluhum ajran fahum min maghramin muthqaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or do you ask of them a payment, so they are by debt burdened down?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or are you asking them for a reward ˹for the message˺ so that they are overburdened by debt?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or have they [knowledge of] the unseen, so they write [it] down?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or do they have access to ˹the Record in˺ the unseen, so they copy it ˹for all to see˺?

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱصۡبِرۡ لِحُكۡمِ رَبِّكَ وَلَا تَكُن كَصَاحِبِ ٱلۡحُوتِ إِذۡ نَادَىٰ وَهُوَ مَكۡظُومٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faisbir lihukmi rabbika wala takun kasahibi alhooti ith nada wahuwa makthoomun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then be patient for the decision of your Lord, [O Muhammad], and be not like the companion of the fish when he called out while he was distressed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So be patient with your Lord’s decree, and do not be like ˹Jonah,˺ the Man of the Whale, who cried out ˹to Allah˺, in total distress.

ayahs.textArabic: لَّوۡلَآ أَن تَدَٰرَكَهُۥ نِعۡمَةٞ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ لَنُبِذَ بِٱلۡعَرَآءِ وَهُوَ مَذۡمُومٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Lawla an tadarakahu niAAmatun min rabbihi lanubitha bialAAarai wahuwa mathmoomun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: If not that a favor from his Lord overtook him, he would have been thrown onto the naked shore while he was censured.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Had he not been shown grace by his Lord, he would have certainly been cast onto the open ˹shore˺, still blameworthy.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And his Lord chose him and made him of the righteous.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then his Lord chose him, making him one of the righteous.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِن يَكَادُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ لَيُزۡلِقُونَكَ بِأَبۡصَٰرِهِمۡ لَمَّا سَمِعُواْ ٱلذِّكۡرَ وَيَقُولُونَ إِنَّهُۥ لَمَجۡنُونٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wain yakadu allatheena kafaroo layuzliqoonaka biabsarihim lamma samiAAoo alththikra wayaqooloona innahu lamajnoonun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, those who disbelieve would almost make you slip with their eyes when they hear the message, and they say, "Indeed, he is mad."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The disbelievers would almost cut you down with their eyes when they hear ˹you recite˺ the Reminder, and say, “He is certainly a madman.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But it is not except a reminder to the worlds.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But it is simply a reminder to the whole world.
